requirements.description: Create document with metadata: number (PLT-5), title, type (SOP/Spec/Protocol/Format/Policy), department, effective date, review frequency, author. File upload (PDF/DOCX) per version.

tests.name: DOC-1 DOC-2 DOC-8 Documents HTTP surface
DOC-1: creates a document via multipart upload — numbered, audited, version 1.0 draft
DOC-1: the uploaded file streams back intact
DOC-1: documents list is tenant-scoped and paginated
DOC-1 DOC-2 DOC-8 DocumentsService
DOC-1: creates a document with metadata, a numbered identity (SOP-QA-001), and an immutable 1.0 draft file
DOC-1: rejects non-PDF/DOCX uploads
DOC-1 DOC-7 DocumentsPage
DOC-1: lists documents with number, status, effective version, and next review date

requirements.description: Version control: major/minor versioning; only one Effective version per document; prior versions auto-marked Superseded and retained read-only.

tests.name: DOC-2 document version transition map
DOC-2: the happy path Draft → Under Review → Under Approval → Effective is allowed
DOC-2/DOC-7: Effective can only become Superseded or Obsolete
DOC-2: terminal states (Superseded/Obsolete/Cancelled) allow NO transitions — retained read-only forever
DOC-2: a draft can never jump straight to Effective — every version passes through review and approval
DOC-1 DOC-2 DOC-8 Documents HTTP surface
DOC-1 DOC-2 DOC-8 DocumentsService
DOC-2: only one Effective version — making a new version Effective auto-Supersedes the prior one (audited)
DOC-2: minor bump versions increment correctly (2.0 → 2.1)
DOC-2: a new version cannot be drafted while another is still in flight
DOC-2: invalid transitions throw and change nothing — prior versions are read-only
DOC-2: document status derives correctly — Effective + new draft = Under Revision

requirements.description: Review → approval workflow via PLT-4 (configurable steps, e.g., Author → Dept Head review → QA Head approval). Each step = e-signature (PLT-3) with meaning.

tests.name: DOC-3: rejections travel backwards — approval → review/draft, review → draft
DOC-3 DOC-6 DOC-7 document lifecycle over HTTP
DOC-3: submit → review e-sign → approval e-sign carries the version to Effective with signature meanings
DOC-3 acceptance (§7.1): a rejection with comment returns the version to the author as Draft, and who/when/why lands in the audit trail
PLT-4 hardening: a user without documents:edit cannot submit a DocumentVersion via the generic workflow endpoint (bypassing DOC-3\'s own gate)
DOC-3 DocumentDetailPage
DOC-3: a draft document shows version history, HistoryTab, and submits for review

requirements.description: Effective documents render/print as **controlled copies**: header block (doc no., version, effective date), footer watermark ("Controlled Copy — verify current version by scanning QR"), and a **QR code** resolving to the live version-check page.

tests.name: DOC-4 DOC-5 controlled copies + QR version check
DOC-4: the controlled copy is a stamped PDF (same page count) and printing it is audited (who/version/when)
DOC-4: a draft version has no controlled copy

requirements.description: QR version check (public-ish page, no PII): scanning a printed copy shows "✔ CURRENT — v3.0 effective 01-Aug-2026" or "✘ OBSOLETE — current version is v4.0". Requires login only to open the full document.

tests.name: DOC-4 DOC-5 controlled copies + QR version check
DOC-5: scanning the CURRENT version needs no login and shows version + effective date
DOC-5 acceptance (§7.1): once v2.0 becomes Effective, scanning the printed v1.0 copy shows OBSOLETE with the current version number
DOC-5: an unknown or non-document code stays a plain 404 — nothing leaks publicly
DOC-5: scanning a CURRENT controlled copy shows the check WITHOUT any login
DOC-5: scanning a stale printed copy shows OBSOLETE with the current version number

requirements.description: Periodic review: background job flags documents past review frequency; QA dashboard widget + notification. Review outcome = "reaffirm" (new review date, minor version) or "revise" (starts revision workflow).

tests.name: DOC-6 periodic-review due-date scanner
DOC-6: registers under a stable key the framework can dedupe on
DOC-6: a document past its review date maps to an OVERDUE finding for the author
DOC-6: a document due within the window (but not past) maps to DUE_SOON, with a dedupeKey stable across daily runs
DOC-3 DOC-6 DOC-7 document lifecycle over HTTP
DOC-6: reaffirm is e-signed, bumps a minor version sharing the same immutable file, and resets the review clock
EQP-4/TRN-5/DOC-6: shows overdue calibrations (filtered from due_soon+overdue), training overdue, and review-due widgets when permitted

requirements.description: Obsolescence workflow with e-signature; obsolete docs excluded from user-facing search but retained and auditable.

tests.name: DOC-2/DOC-7: Effective can only become Superseded or Obsolete
DOC-3 DOC-6 DOC-7 document lifecycle over HTTP
DOC-7: obsolescence requires a fresh e-signature, hides the document from default search, and retains it auditable
DOC-1 DOC-7 DocumentsPage

requirements.description: Equipment master: code (PLT-5), name, make/model/serial, location (room), department, criticality (GMP-critical Y/N), status (Active / Under Maintenance / Under Qualification / Retired), install date.

tests.name: EQP-1 equipment status transition map
EQP-1: Active can move to Under Maintenance, Under Qualification, Retired, or (EQP-5) Do Not Use
EQP-1: Under Maintenance / Under Qualification return to Active or go to Retired
EQP-1: Retired is terminal — no transitions out (Iron Rule 3)
EQP-1: Under Maintenance cannot jump directly to Under Qualification
EQP-1 EQP-2 EQP-3 Equipment HTTP surface
EQP-1: creates equipment (audited) with a numbered code
EQP-1: a status transition follows the map and is audited; an invalid one is rejected
EQP-1: list is tenant-scoped and paginated
EQP-1 EQP-2 EQP-3 EquipmentService
EQP-1: creates equipment with a numbered code, ACTIVE by default
EQP-1: rejects creation against an unknown department
EQP-1: status transitions follow the explicit map — Active → Under Maintenance → Active is fine, Retired → Active is not
EQP-1: list supports status/department filters and search, tenant-scoped
EQP-1 EquipmentDetailPage
EQP-1/EQP-10: shows equipment metadata, HistoryTab, transitions status through the allowed map, and downloads the history report

requirements.description: Each equipment gets a QR (PLT-7). Printable durable-label PDF (multiple sizes). Scan → mobile status card.

tests.name: EQP-1 EQP-2 EQP-3 Equipment HTTP surface
EQP-2: the QR PNG and label PDF endpoints work for the equipment\'s code (reusing PLT-7)
EQP-1 EQP-2 EQP-3 EquipmentService
EQP-2: every created equipment gets a QR code with a working scan URL

requirements.description: **Status card (on scan):** live calibration status (Valid until DD-MMM / DUE / OVERDUE — color-coded), qualification status, PM due date, current status, last 5 logbook entries, action buttons per user role.

tests.name: EQP-3/EQP-4 calibration status derivation
EQP-3: no due date at all is NOT_SCHEDULED
EQP-1 EQP-2 EQP-3 Equipment HTTP surface
EQP-3: ANY authenticated user can open the status card via the QR resolve + status-card endpoints

EQP-3: the status card reports NOT_SCHEDULED calibration and role-gated action stubs

EQP-3: color-codes an OVERDUE calibration
EQP-3: an authenticated scan of an Equipment code renders the real status card, not the generic stub

requirements.description: Calibration: schedule per equipment (frequency, parameters, tolerance class, internal/external agency); auto-generate due tasks; record results + certificate upload; QA verification sign-off (e-sign). Overdue calibration flips equipment card to OVERDUE and (configurable) blocks usage logging with a warning.

tests.name: EQP-4 EQP-5 Calibration HTTP surface
EQP-4: an engineer creates a calibration schedule for the equipment
EQP-4: the engineer records a PASS calibration result with a certificate; QA verifies it
EQP-4: the calibration-due dashboard lists this equipment
EQP-4 EQP-5 CalibrationService
EQP-4: creates a calibration schedule and reports it back
EQP-4: recording a result requires an existing schedule
EQP-4: a PASS result can be QA-verified and advances the schedule nextDueDate
EQP-4: the calibration-due dashboard lists only DUE_SOON/OVERDUE schedules
EQP-4 equipment-calibration due-date scanner
EQP-4: registers under a stable key
EQP-4: an overdue schedule notifies the department head (not the operator)
EQP-4: a schedule with no configured department head produces no finding
EQP-4: a schedule that is still VALID (far in the future) produces no finding
EQP-4: no schedules produces no findings (and skips the equipment/department lookups)
EQP-4: a DUE_SOON schedule (within 30 days) notifies with a due_soon event
EQP-3/EQP-4 calibration status derivation
EQP-4: a due date in the past is OVERDUE
EQP-4: a due date exactly now is OVERDUE (inclusive boundary)
EQP-4: a due date within 30 days is DUE_SOON
EQP-4: a due date more than 30 days out is VALID
EQP-4/EQP-6: usage logging is blocked when calibration is overdue and the tenant blocks on overdue (default true)
EQP-4/TRN-5/DOC-6: shows overdue calibrations (filtered from due_soon+overdue), training overdue, and review-due widgets when permitted
EQP-4 EQP-5 CalibrationPanel
EQP-4: an operator with no equipment:edit/approve sees records but no schedule/record forms
EQP-4: an engineer can create a calibration schedule
EQP-4: QA can verify a PASS record pending verification
EQP-4: shows a usage-blocked warning when overdue calibration blocks usage

requirements.description: Out-of-tolerance (OOT) handling: failed calibration requires an impact-assessment note and flags the equipment "Do Not Use" until QA disposition (e-sign). (Full deviation linkage arrives in Phase 2 — design the reference field now.)

tests.name: EQP-4 EQP-5 Calibration HTTP surface
EQP-5: a FAIL/OOT result requires an impact-assessment note and immediately sets Do Not Use
EQP-4 EQP-5 CalibrationService
EQP-5: a FAIL/OOT result immediately quarantines the equipment (Do Not Use), before any QA action
EQP-5: QA disposition "release" returns the equipment to Active; nextDueDate is untouched
EQP-5: QA disposition "retain_do_not_use" keeps the equipment quarantined
EQP-1: Active can move to Under Maintenance, Under Qualification, Retired, or (EQP-5) Do Not Use
EQP-5: Do Not Use is structurally reachable/leavable, but only via the calibration flow (service-layer guard, not this map)
EQP-5/EQP-6: a Do Not Use equipment blocks new usage-start logging via the real HTTP path
EQP-5/EQP-6: usage logging is blocked when the equipment is flagged Do Not Use, and again when Retired
EQP-4 EQP-5 CalibrationPanel
EQP-5: QA disposition requires a note before the signature dialog appears
EQP-5: shows a DO NOT USE banner when the equipment is quarantined

requirements.description: **Digital logbook via QR:** authenticated operator scans → logs `Usage start/stop (product/batch ref free-text in v1)`, `Cleaning done (type: routine/full)`, `Breakdown report (description + photo)`. Every entry: auto user + server timestamp; entries immutable — corrections via strike-through-style amendment entry, never edit.

EQP-6: an authenticated operator (no elevated permission) starts and stops a usage session
EQP-6: logs a cleaning entry
EQP-6/EQP-7: a breakdown report (with a photo) auto-creates a maintenance task and notifies the maintenance role

EQP-6: logs a cleaning entry by type
EQP-6/EQP-7: reports a breakdown with a description

requirements.description: Breakdown entry auto-creates a maintenance task assigned to maintenance role; closure requires engineer completion note + (configurable) QA/user verification.

EQP-6/EQP-7: a breakdown report (with a photo) auto-creates a maintenance task and notifies the maintenance role

requirements.description: Qualification records: IQ/OQ/PQ entries with protocol/report file upload, dates, status; requalification due tracking.

requirements.description: Login, JWT, roles, permission matrix, lockout, session policy

PLT-1: JwtAuthGuard allows public routes through without a token
PLT-1: JwtAuthGuard rejects requests without a bearer token
PLT-1: JwtAuthGuard rejects an invalid or expired token
PLT-1: JwtAuthGuard attaches the decoded user to the request for a valid access token

PLT-1: PermissionsGuard denies access when the user lacks the required permission
PLT-1: PermissionsGuard denies access when there is no authenticated user
PLT-1: PermissionsGuard allows access when the user has the required permission
PLT-1: PermissionsGuard allows access when the route requires no permission

PLT-1: TenantGuard allows requests once a tenant context is established
PLT-1: TenantGuard allows public routes through without tenant context

PLT-1: an operator without equipment:create cannot create equipment

PLT-1: POST /auth/login rejects an incorrect password with AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS
PLT-1: POST /auth/login returns a validation error for a malformed email

PLT-1: POST /auth/refresh rejects a malformed refresh token
PLT-1: POST /auth/refresh returns a validation error when refreshToken is missing

PLT-1: user document requires a tenantId
PLT-1: login only matches a user within the given tenantId (tenant isolation)
PLT-1: login rejects a tenantId that does not match the user (tenant isolation)
PLT-1: login locks the account after the configured number of consecutive failed attempts
PLT-1: a successful login resets the failed-attempt counter
PLT-1: refresh rotates the refresh token and rejects reuse of the previous one
PLT-1: refresh rejects a malformed token
PLT-1: login flags mustChangePassword when the password has expired
PLT-1: login does not flag mustChangePassword for a recently changed password
PLT-1: access token embeds the role permission matrix

PLT-1: redirects unauthenticated users to /login
PLT-1 LoginPage
PLT-1: submits credentials and navigates to the home page on success

requirements.description: Single `auditEvents` writer used by all modules; per-record history API; export

PLT-2: does not record when there is no authenticated user on the request

PLT-2: editing the record writes a field-level old→new diff into its history

requirements.description: Configurable multi-step approval flows (sequential steps, role-based assignees, reject-back-to-step, comments). Reuse Patent CRM design patterns.

tests.name: Phase 0 gate — PLT-1 PLT-2 PLT-3 PLT-4 PLT-5 PLT-6 PLT-7 PLT-8 integration
PLT-4 + PLT-6: submitting routes it into the 2-step approval and notifies the first assignee
PLT-3 + PLT-4: two real e-signatures carry the record to APPROVED; session-only signing is rejected
PLT-4 hardening: a user without documents:edit cannot submit a DocumentVersion via the generic workflow endpoint (bypassing DOC-3\'s own gate)
verifyAndConsumeSigningToken (also reused in-process by PLT-4 WorkflowService)

PLT-4: SUBMIT is only allowed from DRAFT
PLT-4: APPROVE/REJECT/REASSIGN are only allowed from IN_PROGRESS
PLT-4: no actions are allowed from the terminal APPROVED status

PLT-4: assertWorkflowActionAllowed() does not throw for a valid transition

PLT-4: creates a workflow template
PLT-4: a non-admin cannot create a workflow template
PLT-4: full happy path via HTTP — submit, two step approvals with real e-signatures, my-pending-tasks reflects each step

PLT-4: full happy path — submit, then two step approvals (two signatures) reach APPROVED
PLT-4: a non-assignee (wrong role) is blocked from approving
PLT-4: reject at the first step returns the instance to DRAFT (the author)
PLT-4: reject at a later step returns to its configured earlier step, not necessarily all the way to draft
PLT-4: reassign is audited with a reason and changes who may act on the step
PLT-4: invalid transitions throw — cannot re-submit an in-progress instance or act on an approved one
PLT-4: workflow instances are tenant-isolated — each tenant gets its own independent instance
PLT-4: my-pending-tasks lists instances awaiting the actor\'s role, and reassigned instances awaiting the specific user
PLT-4 WorkflowStepper
PLT-4: shows a draft message when the instance has not been submitted
PLT-4: shows the current step name and position while in progress
PLT-4: shows an Approved badge once the instance is fully approved
PLT-4: shows pending approvals with no permission gate, even with no other module access
PLT-2 PLT-3 PLT-4 PLT-5 PLT-7: shows number, signatures, history, QR block, and submits for approval
PLT-4 PendingTasksPage
PLT-4: shows a message when there is nothing awaiting the user
PLT-4: lists pending tasks with a link to review each one
PLT-4 WorkflowInstancePage
PLT-4: approves the current step after a successful signature challenge
PLT-4: rejects with a mandatory comment

requirements.description: Tenant-configurable document/entity numbering (e.g., `SOP-QA-001`, `EQP-0042`, `TRN-2026-0113`) with no gaps/duplicates

tests.name: Phase 0 gate — PLT-1 PLT-2 PLT-3 PLT-4 PLT-5 PLT-6 PLT-7 PLT-8 integration
PLT-5 + PLT-7: creating a record assigns TR-0001 from the numbering service and mints its QR identity
PLT-5 NumberingService
PLT-5: generates a simple padded sequence (EQP-0042 style)
PLT-5: generates a department-token sequence (SOP-QA-001 style), independent per department
PLT-5: generates a yearly-reset sequence (TRN-2026-0113 style)
PLT-5: rejects generation for an entityType with no configured scheme
PLT-5: rejects generation when the scheme requires a departmentCode and none is given
PLT-5: numbering is tenant-isolated — two tenants each get their own sequence starting at 1
PLT-5: 50 concurrent generateNumber calls produce a gapless, duplicate-free sequence
PLT-5: updateScheme() applies changes and reports a before/after snapshot
PLT-2 PLT-3 PLT-4 PLT-5 PLT-7: shows number, signatures, history, QR block, and submits for approval

requirements.description: Template-based email; events: assigned, due-soon, overdue, approved, rejected

tests.name: Phase 0 gate — PLT-1 PLT-2 PLT-3 PLT-4 PLT-5 PLT-6 PLT-7 PLT-8 integration
PLT-4 + PLT-6: submitting routes it into the 2-step approval and notifies the first assignee
PLT-6: the submitting author is notified of the final approval
PLT-6 due-date scanner framework
PLT-6: scanners register into the framework; a duplicate key is rejected
PLT-6: the daily run turns scanner findings into notifications and records one scan-run per tenant/scanner/day
PLT-6: re-running the scan on the same day is idempotent — the scanner is not called again and nothing new is created
PLT-6: the next day the scanner runs again, but stable dedupeKeys still prevent duplicate notifications for the same fact
PLT-6: each tenant is scanned separately, and the run date follows the TENANT timezone
PLT-6: formatDateInTimezone renders the tenant-local calendar day
PLT-6 EmailDeliveryService
PLT-6: sendForNotification() mails the recipient and stamps emailedAt; re-delivery is a no-op (safe job retries)
PLT-6: sendDailyDigests() batches all unsent notifications into ONE email per user for digest-mode tenants only
PLT-6 FileMailer transport
PLT-6: appends one JSON line per message to the outbox file
PLT-6 Notifications HTTP surface
PLT-6: notification endpoints reject unauthenticated requests
PLT-6: GET /notifications returns only the caller\'s own notifications — not other users\', not other tenants\'
PLT-6: unread-count and mark-read work end-to-end, and mark-read writes an audit event
PLT-6: submitting a workflow via HTTP produces a task_assigned notification for the first step\'s assignee (listener wiring)
PLT-6 NotificationsService
PLT-6: notify() creates a per-user log entry and writes an audit event for it
PLT-6: notify() with the same dedupeKey for the same user is idempotent — one entry, second call returns null
PLT-6: the same dedupeKey for two different recipients creates one entry each
PLT-6: immediate-mode tenants get an email enqueued; digest-mode tenants do not
PLT-6: list() returns only the requesting user\'s notifications within their tenant (tenant isolation)
PLT-6: markRead() flips only the targeted own notifications, and unreadOnly listing reflects it
PLT-6 workflow event -> notification mapping
PLT-6: submit maps to task_assigned for every ACTIVE user holding the first step\'s role
PLT-6: a non-final approval maps to task_assigned for the NEXT step\'s role
PLT-6: the final approval maps to an approved notification for the submitter
PLT-6: reject back to DRAFT maps to a rejected notification (with the comment) for the submitter only
PLT-6: reject to an EARLIER STEP notifies the submitter (rejected) AND re-assigns that step\'s role (task_assigned)
PLT-6: reassign maps to task_assigned for the substitute user only — not the step\'s whole role
PLT-6 NotificationBell
PLT-6: shows the unread count badge and lists unread notifications when opened
PLT-6: mark-all-read calls the API and refreshes the badge

requirements.description: Generate/resolve short codes → entity; label PDF sheets; scan landing router `/s/:code`

tests.name: Phase 0 gate — PLT-1 PLT-2 PLT-3 PLT-4 PLT-5 PLT-6 PLT-7 PLT-8 integration
PLT-5 + PLT-7: creating a record assigns TR-0001 from the numbering service and mints its QR identity
PLT-7: the QR code resolves to the record for tenant users — and its mobile scan URL is stable
EQP-2: the QR PNG and label PDF endpoints work for the equipment\'s code (reusing PLT-7)
PLT-7 QR HTTP surface
PLT-7: an admin mints a QR code for an entity, and the creation is audited
PLT-7: a non-admin cannot mint codes over HTTP
PLT-7: any authenticated user in the tenant resolves the code to its entity
PLT-7: a user from another tenant scanning the code gets 404 (cross-tenant scan blocked)
PLT-7: unauthenticated resolution is rejected
PLT-7: the QR PNG endpoint streams a PNG image
PLT-7: the label PDF endpoint renders single and A4-grid PDFs
PLT-7 QrService
PLT-7: mints an opaque short code and builds the /s/:code scan URL
PLT-7: get-or-create is idempotent — one code per entity, re-posting returns the same code
PLT-7: a code collision is survived by regenerating — the unique index is the arbiter
PLT-7: resolve() maps the code back to its entity within the owning tenant
PLT-7: a cross-tenant scan is blocked — a foreign code resolves as NOT_FOUND, indistinguishable from an unknown code
PLT-7: generatePng() returns a PNG image for the tenant\'s own code
PLT-2 PLT-3 PLT-4 PLT-5 PLT-7: shows number, signatures, history, QR block, and submits for approval
PLT-7 mobile scan landing (/s/:code)
PLT-7: an unauthenticated scan redirects to login PRESERVING the scanned target
PLT-7: an authenticated scan of a non-equipment entity resolves and shows the persistent "Logged in as" banner (generic stub)
PLT-7: a code the tenant cannot resolve shows an error card, not a crash

requirements.description: Tenant provisioning, user CRUD, department master, role assignment

tests.name: Phase 0 gate — PLT-1 PLT-2 PLT-3 PLT-4 PLT-5 PLT-6 PLT-7 PLT-8 integration
PLT-8: tenant isolation holds end-to-end — an outsider can neither read the record nor resolve its QR
PLT-8 tenant isolation: another tenant cannot see this equipment calibration schedule
PLT-8 tenant isolation: another tenant cannot download this equipment history report
PLT-8 tenant isolation: another tenant cannot see or scan this equipment
PLT-8 tenant isolation: another tenant cannot read this equipment logbook or maintenance tasks
PLT-8 tenant isolation: another tenant cannot read this equipment qualification/PM data
PLT-8 tenant isolation: an outsider sees no assignments and cannot reach this tenant\'s training data
PLT-8 DepartmentService
PLT-8: creates a department with an uppercased code
PLT-8: rejects a duplicate department code within the same tenant
PLT-8: the same department code is allowed across different tenants (tenant isolation)
PLT-8: update() deactivates a department instead of deleting it (Iron Rule 3)
PLT-8: list() only returns departments for the given tenant

PLT-8: POST /tenants rejects a non-platform-admin session
PLT-8: provisions a tenant, its admin can log in, and manage departments/users end-to-end
PLT-8: GET /tenants/:id rejects a non-platform-admin session

PLT-8: provisionTenant() creates the tenant, a full-permission Tenant Admin role, and the first user
PLT-8: provisionTenant() rejects a duplicate slug
PLT-8: provisionTenant() applies partial settings overrides on top of defaults
PLT-8: updateSettings() applies a partial update and reports before/after
PLT-8: listTenants() and findById() return provisioned tenants
PLT-8 UserAdminService
PLT-8: createUser() hashes the password and assigns the given role
PLT-8: createUser() rejects a weak password
PLT-8: createUser() rejects a duplicate email within the same tenant
PLT-8: createUser() rejects an unknown roleId
PLT-8: updateUser() reassigns role/department and reports before/after
PLT-8: updateUser() deactivating a user bumps tokenVersion to invalidate sessions (Iron Rule 3: no hard delete)
PLT-8: listUsers() paginates and is scoped to the tenant (tenant isolation)
PLT-8: listRoles() returns only the tenant\'s own roles
PLT-8 PlatformAdminRoute
PLT-8: redirects a signed-in but non-platform-admin user to the home page
PLT-8: allows a platform-admin user through
PLT-8 DepartmentsPage
PLT-8: lists departments and creates a new one

requirements.description: External calibration agency management: agency master, certificate registry, agency-wise due list.

Material status QR label: generated against a material/lot entry with status Quarantine/Approved/Rejected; scan shows status + QA sign-off details. **View + status-change by QA only. No quantities. No movement.** (See Non-Goals.)
Assessments: optional MCQ quiz per document; pass mark configurable; failed = retrain.
Classroom/external training records: manual entry with certificate upload (inductions, external GMP trainings).
In-app editor / templates. v1 = file upload only.
Spare-parts linkage; utilization analytics.
Trainer-led sessions with attendance capture via QR (attendee scans session QR to mark presence).
